title: Peanut Butter & Chickpea Stew
description: A quick, creamy one-pot stew where peanut butter and tomato sauce melt together into a rich, comforting sauce for chickpeas, vegetables and rice.

ingredients.name: Tomato sauce (100% tomato, no added sugar)
Fresh or frozen vegetables (broccoli, green beans, spinach...)

steps: Cook the rice according to the packet instructions in salted water, then drain and set aside.
Meanwhile, finely chop the onion and garlic. Heat a little oil in a pot over medium heat and sauté them for 3-4 minutes until soft.
Stir in the paprika and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant, then add the tomato sauce and peanut butter. Stir until the peanut butter has melted into the sauce.
Add the chickpeas and the vegetables (if using frozen, no need to thaw first). Simmer for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender and the sauce has thickened.
Season with the salt and pepper, adjust to taste, and serve hot over the rice.
footnote: Quantities here are sized for about 2 servings — scale up using the servings control if you're cooking for more. Using dried chickpeas instead of tinned: soak 125 g overnight and simmer until tender before adding to the stew, which will add roughly an hour to the prep. Spice amounts (salt, pepper, paprika) are a starting point — adjust to taste.
